senses.definition: a type of sport where two teams, with eleven players each, try to kick a ball into a specific area to win points

examples.x: I like to watch soccer matches on TV and support my favorite team.
She joined a soccer league to compete against other teams.
They practice their soccer skills every day after school.
We cheer loudly for our favorite soccer team during the match.

senses.definition: a sport played by two teams of 11 players who carry, throw, or kick an oval ball on a rectangular field
a game played by two teams of thirteen or fifteen players, who kick or carry an oval ball over the other team’s line to score points

examples.x: He started playing rugby at a young age.
Our city is hosting a rugby tournament next month.
Rugby requires strength and good teamwork.
They train for rugby three times a week.
We are watching a rugby match on TV tonight.

senses.definition: a game that is mostly played outside where each person uses a special stick to hit a small white ball into a number of holes with the least number of swings

examples.x: Golf is often considered a relaxing sport.
He practices his golf swing at the driving range.
The golf tournament attracted many spectators.
They are planning a charity golf event next month.

senses.definition: a type of sport where two teams, with often five players each, try to throw a ball through a net that is hanging from a ring and gain points

examples.x: He enjoys playing basketball with his friends at the local park on weekends.
Basketball is his favorite sport to play in his free time.
The coach emphasized teamwork as the key to success in basketball.
He started playing basketball in high school and eventually earned a scholarship to play in college.
The players practiced their basketball skills for the upcoming tournament.

senses.definition: a type of sport in which two teams of 6 players try to hit a ball over a net and into the other team's side

examples.x: She enjoys the teamwork and strategy involved in playing volleyball.
She joined a local volleyball league to compete against other teams.
Volleyball is an exciting sport where teams try to score points by hitting the ball over the net.
We are learning the rules of volleyball to participate in a friendly game.
We cheer loudly for our school's volleyball team during their matches.

senses.definition: a game played by two teams of eleven players who try to get points by hitting the ball with a wooden bat and running between two sets of vertical wooden sticks

examples.x: I enjoy watching cricket games on television.
My brother taught me how to play cricket.
The cricket team practiced every day after school.

senses.definition: a game similar to hockey, played by two teams of eleven players on a field or grass, using sticks and a ball

examples.x: Field hockey requires speed and agility.
Field hockey uses a hard ball and curved sticks.
His dream is to become a professional field hockey player.
Our school has a fantastic field hockey team.
The field hockey match will take place on Saturday.

senses.definition: a game played on a field with two teams, each consisting of ten players using long-handled sticks with a net to throw, carry, and catch the ball
a team sport played on horseback where players use mallets to hit a ball into the opposing team's goal
a game played with a bat and ball by two teams of 9 players who try to hit the ball and then run around four bases before the other team can return the ball

examples.x: Baseball is a popular sport in the United States.
He loves to play baseball every weekend with his friends.
In baseball, the aim is to hit the ball and then run around the bases.
She learned to play baseball when she was only seven years old.

senses.definition: a sport or game in which a player rolls a ball down a lane with the aim of knocking over as many pins as possible at the other end of the lane

examples.x: Bowling is a game of precision and timing.
Bowling is a popular indoor sport, especially during the winter.

senses.definition: a traditional Irish team sport where players use a wooden stick to hit a small ball between goals and over a crossbar for points

examples.x: Hurling is one of the oldest and fastest field sports in the world.
Players wear helmets and protective gear due to the fast-paced nature of hurling.

senses.definition: a team sport played indoors with plastic sticks and a lightweight ball, resembling indoor hockey

examples.x: The floorball team practiced passing drills and shooting exercises to improve their skills and coordination on the court.
She joined a recreational floorball league to stay active and have fun playing a sport she enjoyed.
Floorball is played with lightweight plastic sticks and a hollow ball on a court with goals at each end, requiring speed, agility, and teamwork.
The floorball tournament drew teams from across the region to compete for the championship title in an exciting display of skill and athleticism.
He scored a hat trick in the floorball match, leading his team to victory with his precise shooting and strategic playmaking.

senses.definition: a team sport where players pass a ball without dribbling to shoot it into a raised netted goal

examples.x: She loves playing netball every weekend with her friends.
They scored a goal in the last minute to win the netball match.
I watched a netball match on TV last night, and the final score was so close.

senses.definition: an indoor game for two teams of players each trying to throw a ball with their hands to the opponent's goal

examples.x: Playing handball competitively demands both physical stamina and strategic thinking on the court.
She joined the school's handball club to improve her agility and coordination skills.
The championship match in handball drew a large crowd of enthusiastic spectators.
Learning the rules and tactics of handball helped him appreciate the sport's complexity and excitement.
She has been practicing handball for several years.

senses.definition: a type of football played in Canada with distinct rules, a larger field, and teams of twelve players
a fast-paced ball sport played between two teams of 18 players on an oval field
a sport played with a rattan ball where players use their feet, head, knees, and chest to hit the ball over a net

examples.x: They set up a net to play sepak takraw in the park.
The school introduced sepak takraw as a new sport activity.
The sepak takraw tournament drew a large crowd.
She learned the rules of sepak takraw quickly.
Sepak takraw is popular in Southeast Asia.

senses.definition: a mixed-gender team sport similar to basketball and netball, where players score by shooting a ball into a high net

examples.x: She joined the school's korfball team last year.
The new gym has facilities for playing korfball.
They watched a professional korfball tournament on TV.
They practiced korfball every Saturday afternoon.

senses.definition: a team sport combining elements of handball and volleyball, where players score by bouncing a ball off a rebound net

examples.x: He scored the winning point in the tchoukball game.
They set up a tchoukball net in the park for practice.
The tchoukball team trained hard for the upcoming tournament.
Tchoukball matches are fast-paced and exciting to watch.
She enjoys playing tchoukball with her friends on weekends.

senses.definition: a team sport where players hit a ball over a net using their fists or arms

examples.x: The coach emphasized teamwork during fistball practice.
She excels at playing fistball with her local team.
They joined a fistball league to stay active and meet new people.

senses.definition: a team sport designed for visually impaired athletes, where players try to throw a ball with bells into the opponent's goal

examples.x: The goalball court is divided into three sections for each team.
Watching goalball competitions is both exciting and inspiring.
Goalball tournaments attract athletes from around the world.
She volunteered to coach the local goalball team.

senses.definition: a sport played mainly in Ireland, combining elements of soccer, rugby, and basketball

examples.x: He plays Gaelic football for his local club.
He dreams of representing his counrty in Gaelic football.

senses.definition: a pass that covers a significant distance to reach a teammate, often used to quickly advance the ball or puck

examples.x: He made a long pass to the striker.
The long pass sailed over the midfield.
The long pass reached the wide receiver.
They practiced the long pass in training.
Her long pass changed the game's momentum.

senses.definition: a fast-paced game that combines elements of handball, soccer, and basketball

examples.x: The new speed-ball court was the most popular spot in the recreation center.
She scored the winning goal in the final seconds of the speed-ball match.
During gym class, the students enjoyed a competitive game of speed-ball.
Speed-ball combines the best aspects of several different sports.
The speed-ball players wore special shoes for better traction on the court.

senses.definition: to move the ball while maintaining control or possession of it

examples.x: The running back carried the football through a crowd of defenders.
She carried the soccer ball past midfield with skillful dribbling.
She carries the puck skillfully down the ice.
The winger carries the puck into the opponent's zone.
He carries the ball with great speed and control.
